title: Vote transfers between France's right and far right are increasingly common
subhead: By alternately voting for either the far right or the mainstream right to defeat the left, voters in the recent municipal elections have illustrated the two parties' ideological convergence.

Cuba's escalating health crisis is 'deeply concerning,' says WHO chief
subhead: World Health Organization Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, on X Wednesday, described the 'immense turbulence' going on in Cuba, with 'energy shortages that have been affecting health.' Daily power outages of up to 20 hours are the norm in parts of the Caribbean island, which lacks the fuel needed to generate power.

title: Night train service resumes between Paris and Berlin
subhead: The Belgian-Dutch cooperative European Sleeper is reviving the symbolic rail link between the French and German capitals that was discontinued by SNCF in December 2025.

title: The sensuality of Henri Rousseau's paintings at the Musée de l'Orangerie
subhead: The exhibition 'Henri Rousseau: A Painter's Ambition' offers a stimulating reinterpretation of the artist's work, highlighting his rigor and discipline, far removed from the cliché of the 'naïve' or 'Sunday painter.'

title: Thierry Breton: 'A golden rule limiting the public deficit to 1% of GDP by 2032 must be enshrined in the French Constitution'
subhead: A country that devotes an ever-increasing share of its resources to funding its past is no longer in control of its destiny, says the former finance minister, while outlining his plan to put a stop to the rise in public debt.
